original text:
IHG's Gamified First Quarter Promo is Out, What Did You Get?
The new first quarter promotion from IHG Rewards Club is out, running January 1 through April 15.
IHG Rewards Club has moved to ‘gamified' promotions with different pieces meant to incentivize behavior among members  -  with the specifics driven by data about your activity so far, like whether you're a co-brand credit card holder or an elite.
And they're back with... Accelerate. Sign up and you'll see your customized offer.
I'm not a frequent IHG Rewards Club guest. My offer is pretty weak, although staying once in January for 3 nights should earn me 8000 bonus points. Most of my points are based on staying progressively more nights. I don't have anything for different brands, for their credit card, or for achieving all the other tasks like some will.
I value IHG Rewards Club points at 6/10ths of a cent apiece. That makes 35,000 bonus points worth $210. That's not huge value for the 18 nights it would take me to get there. "Better than a hole in the head" as my grandfather used to say, but not close to enough to get me to shift my business which is why I won't likely earn more than 2000, 3000, or 8000 points off this promotion if I earn any at all.
Your offer may be better, you may earn a bunch of points if you'll stay at IHG properties anyway, and it always makes sense to register because you won't remember to do it later when you come to find out you have some stays with the brand after all.
I got basically the same crappy offer as above...stayed a total of 3 nights over the last Accelerate promo period. Somehow they think I would suddenly spend EIGHTEEN days over the next.
I love that they do this, and particularly how there's almost always something for just staying 1-2 nights. For those of us (i.e. virtually all of us) who aren't going to spend 25 nights in a Hyatt during one quarter, it's nice to have offers that are achievable.
I wonder if reward stays count towards meeting the ‘challenges'?
In the words of the immortal Charlie Brown, I got a rock.
If I book and stay once (for 3,000) in the DC major market (for 2,400) in January (for 1,000) and pay with IHG credit (for 1,500) I will complete 3 of 4 (for 17,100). This makes 25,000 total for one night.
As to the 8 nights (for 16,000) - forget it.
So, not the most points I ever got from IHG, but the easiest.
I got the same offer as Gary, except for the 2nd and 3rd threshold: I got 1,000 an 6,000, Gary got 2,000 and 5,000.
2 HI Express stays (8,000), one of which in January (1,000), using IHG card (1,500) gets the achievement bonus (17,500) for a total of 31,000. 3 more nights gets me another 10,000.
Where do I find out what my offer is?
this works for me as i booked a 2 night stay at an intercontinental in asia next month.
i paid a prepaid rate for this intercontinental stay with my chase ihg card. this charge has been posted already. will this satisfy "Spend on your IHG® Rewards Club Credit Card" offer?
I probably won't start on Accelerate until March, as I'll be trying for Hyatt Globalist in 25 stays in January and February. Then after April, I'll get started on the amazing deal of Starwood Platinum in 25 stays this year to secure Marriott Platinum, which normally requires 75 stays.
1 paid, and 1 using points and cash?
The advice to always sign up for these types of promotions is always a good idea.
I certainly do sign up and sometimes I find myself meeting all the requirements without deliberately trying to do so. One year I discovered that I got a huge bonus from an IHG promotion that I had totally forgotten about.
The reason it has been easy for me is because I got to Asia frequently and IHG has such a large footprint there that I end up staying at an IHG hotel most of the time.
I'd rather buy the 41,000 points than deal with this promo. Where are the ones with 4 free nights?
@ steven k, re:pre-paying before the promo started for a stay during the promo period also failed for me on my pay using IHG credit card challenge. Maybe it's just me.
My experience has been that IHG allows themselves to re-interpret the rules for these Accelerate promotions after the fact. My fall Accelerate offered 30,000 points for a second stay as offer #2. So I did a mattress run at the Crown Plaza Dulles for $75/night as my second stay of the quarter. Turns out the fine print was later changed to "earn double points, up to a MAXIMUM of 30,000 points per account, for your second stay". I got about 1,800 points instead of 30,000.
I "live" in hotels (around 300 nights per year), so it's been pretty easy to hit these IHG bonuses.
IHG keeps offering me 500 points to download their app and sign in with it. I'm not downloading that damned app, unless I can use it on a desktop without any hint that a phone even exists.
DNN on Why is Chase's Sapphire Preferred Offering a 60,000 Point Initial Bonus?

original text:
HOW ARE WE NOT OPPOSSING TRADITIONAL KOLLELS?
The core of the Kollel is made up of high level avreichim who study full-time. They chose to be with us in the morning for the quality of the learning and for the framework, even though our institution only functions part-time. In the afternoon they study in other structures.
HOW DO THE MISHKAN AARON INSTITUTIONS BRING ABOUT A NEW SOLUTION?
Both the Torah world like the working world have always functioned on a full-time schedule, only those who study all day receive a stipend.
Today the working world has evolved by allowing more and more people to work part-time. The Kollel offers a transitional solution. By providing payment for the half-day study, we notice that many make the choice to sit and study.
WHAT ARE THE TARGET POPULATION?
All sections of society are represented, with no age restrictions. All feel concerned about the project.
All who were from the Torah world, who left the kollelim to join the working world.
Workers or self-employed who would like to dedicate some time to Torah study.
WHY DO THOSE WHO ARE WORKING RECEIVE A STUDY GRANT?
In Israel at the moment salaries are low. Any help is greatly appreciated to allow families to live more decently. Additionally, the study grant requires from each individual seriousness and diligence.
Short-term:consolidate the existing morning structure.
Medium-term:open an identical afternoon structure.
Long-term: develop the project and replicate the model in the country's large urban areas and business centers. Simply, wherever the need is felt.
